<type>file</type>
<content>1: #!/usr/bin/env sh
2: set -eu
3: 
4: . "$(CDPATH= cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd)/paperclip-env.sh"
5: 
6: RUNNING_PID=""
7: 
8: if [ -f "$PAPERCLIP_PID_FILE" ]; then
9:   PID=$(cat "$PAPERCLIP_PID_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)
10:   if [ -n "${PID:-}" ] && kill -0 "$PID" 2>/dev/null; then
11:     RUNNING_PID="$PID"
12:   fi
13: fi
14: 
15: if [ -z "$RUNNING_PID" ] && command -v lsof >/dev/null 2>&1; then
16:   PID=$(lsof -tiTCP:"$PAPERCLIP_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)
17:   if [ -n "${PID:-}" ]; then
18:     RUNNING_PID="$PID"
19:   fi
20: fi
21: 
22: if [ -n "$RUNNING_PID" ]; then
23:   echo "Status: running"
24:   echo "PID: $RUNNING_PID"
25: else
26:   echo "Status: stopped"
27: fi
28: 
29: echo "URL: $PAPERCLIP_URL"
30: echo "Config: $PAPERCLIP_CONFIG"
31: echo "Home: $PAPERCLIP_HOME"
32: echo "Launch log: $PAPERCLIP_LAUNCH_LOG"
33: 
34: if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
35:   HEALTH=$(curl -fsS "$PAPERCLIP_URL/api/health" 2>/dev/null || true)
36:   if [ -n "$HEALTH" ]; then
37:     echo "Health: $HEALTH"
38:   fi
39: fi
40: 
41: # Show the latest available database backup for quick health check
42: BACKUPS_DIR="$PAPERCLIP_HOME/instances/$PAPERCLIP_INSTANCE_ID/data/backups"
43: if [ -d "$BACKUPS_DIR" ]; then
44:   LATEST_BACKUP=$(ls -1 "$BACKUPS_DIR" 2>/dev/null | sort -r | head -n 1 || true)
45:   if [ -n "$LATEST_BACKUP" ]; then
46:     echo "Latest backup: $LATEST_BACKUP"
47:   fi
48: fi

(End of file - total 48 lines)
</content>","title":"paperclip-demo/scripts/paperclip-status.sh","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env sh
set -eu

. "$(CDPATH= cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd)/paperclip-env.sh"

RUNNING_PID=""

if [ -f "$PAPERCLIP_PID_FILE" ]; then
  PID=$(cat "$PAPERCLIP_PID_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "${PID:-}" ] && kill -0 "$PID" 2>/dev/null; then
    RUNNING_PID="$PID"
  fi
fi

if [ -z "$RUNNING_PID" ] && command -v lsof >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  PID=$(lsof -tiTCP:"$PAPERCLIP_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)
  if [ -n "${PID:-}" ]; then
    RUNNING_PID="$PID"
  fi
fi","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"time":{"start":1774284107129,"end":1774284107166}},"metadata":{"opencode":{"itemId":"fc_02629ad0279501210169c16d4a754481978178821fe5e32b29"}}}}

{"type":"text","timestamp":1774284146115,"sessionID":"ses_2e5203129ffe06dhA2xE3xJABS","part":{"id":"prt_d1b9378bc001W04AvF5Dfs199F","sessionID":"ses_2e5203129ffe06dhA2xE3xJABS","messageID":"msg_d1b932b5a001i09j53p7OIczkO","type":"text","text":"Plan to continue Paperclip work

Current context
- Backup rotation script added and committed (root path): paperclip-demo/scripts/paperclip-backup-rotate.sh
- Status script currently reports the latest backup filename but not a human-friendly timestamp
- Backups exist under .paperclip-home/instances/demo/data/backups, with a recent file paperclip-20260323-204108.sql

What I’ll implement next (concise plan)
1) Backups retention improvements
- Add env-driven retention and safe dry-run
  - NEW: PAPERCLIP_BACKUP_RETENTION (default 7)
  - NEW: PAPERCLIP_BACKUP_DRY_RUN (default false)
- Behavior
  - If DRY_RUN is set (or true), print which backups would be deleted but do not remove them
  - If not dry-run, delete the oldest backups beyond the retention count
- Rationale: safer, configurable retention suitable for dev/prod parity

2) Status output enhancements
- Extend the backup bytes printed by the status script
  - Print a human-friendly backup time derived from the filename paperclip-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.sql (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)
  - Leave existing health/output intact

3) Documentation
- Add a short README snippet (or update a docs section) detailing:
  - How backups are retained
  - How to configure PAPERCLIP_BACKUP_RETENTION and PAPERCLIP_BACKUP_DRY_RUN
  - How to run the rotation manually and what to expect

4) Optional small test helper
- A tiny test script to simulate backups in a temp dir and verify rotation behavior for a given retention

What I’ll deliver
- Patch series to:
  - paperclip-backup-rotate.sh (root script) to support RETENTION and DRY_RUN
  - paperclip-status.sh (root script under scripts/) to print a formatted backup time
  - A README snippet documenting the retention policy and how to configure
- A quick verification guide (commands you can run locally)

How you can validate locally (short)
- Check current status and latest backup:
  - Run: bash paperclip-demo/scripts/paperclip-status.sh (or the equivalent in your env)
  - Expect: Health line and a line like “Latest backup: paperclip-20260323-204108.sql” plus a “Backup time: 2026-03-23 20:41:08”
- Trigger rotation (dry-run):
  - PAPERCLIP_BACKUP_RETENTION=7 PAPERCLIP_BACKUP_DRY_RUN=1 bash paperclip-demo/scripts/paperclip-backup-rotate.sh
  - Expect: prints which backups would be deleted, no files removed
- Trigger rotation (actual):
  - PAPERCLIP_BACKUP_RETENTION=7 bash paperclip-demo/scripts/paperclip-backup-rotate.sh
  - Expect: deletes oldest backups beyond the retention

If you’re happy with this plan, I’ll implement the changes and commit them as a small, cohesive patch set, then summarize exactly what changed and how to test.","time":{"start":1774284146112,"end":1774284146112},"metadata":{"opencode":{"itemId":"msg_0b90d0eab2bcd2f80169c16d6e2fa48193ae1e5fec0aba82fc"}}}}
